name: Berry Gordy
description: American record producer, founder of Motown Records
abstract: Berry Gordy III (born November 28, 1929), also known as Berry Gordy Jr., is an American retired record executive, record producer, songwriter, film producer, and television producer. He is best known as the founder of the Motown record label and its subsidiaries, which was the highest-earning African-American business for decades.

works_created.t: You've Made Me So Very Happy

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: we grew up together... I have a good relationship with Berry Gordy
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: this guy Berry Gordy and some other people that knew about me... they wanted me to come out there... when I went out there and I started working as a conductor, I was conducting shows, I was conducting Stevie Wonder and the Four Tops
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing a lot of stuff for Motown then, Smokey Robinson and Berry Gordy and all this kind of stuff. So I wanted to do — I was doing Motown sessions as a producer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know whether Berry Gordy was there with the Tops and their manager come in and see that there’s this little chick out there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: In fact we did one record date, I think, that had Berry Gordy before he really got into his soul thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: No mention of Berry Gordy in the interviewee's response; only mentioned in interviewer's note.
